data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "Drought-induced crop failure caused mass migration of Sea Peoples who overwhelmed palace economies militarily but were absorbed by Egypt's Nile-irrigated surplus", "mechanism": "Climate anomaly → subsistence crisis → armed migration → differential military vulnerability based on agricultural resilience and buffer capacity", "scope": {"covers": ["Mycenaean Greece", "Hittite Anatolia", "Ugarit", "Levantine city-states", "Sea Peoples invasions", "Egyptian survival"], "excludes": ["Minoan collapse (earlier)", "Assyrian survival", "Babylonian continuity"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "No climate anomaly detected in proxy records for 1200-1150 BC Eastern Mediterranean", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}, {"case": "Sea Peoples attested as established mercenary forces within palace economies decades before collapse, not external migrants", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}, {"case": "Egyptian agricultural system also collapsed; survival due solely to military factors", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "Integrates climate and migration narratives but centers differential absorption capacity; Egypt's Nile irrigation created surplus buffer that Anatolian rain-fed and Greek mixed economies lacked"}
